sections.paragraph: βαρύς, εῖα, ύ [of Sanskrit origin, cp. Lat. gravis ‘heavy, weighty’] the NT always uses the central idea of being physically ‘heavy in weight’ in a transferred sense: burdensome 1 J 5:3; impressive 2 Cor 10:10; serious charges Ac 25:7; in imagery of wolves rapacious 20:29.—Cp. τὰ βαρύτερα τοῦ νόμου the more important aspects of the law Mt 23:23.
